_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **MONITORING** — Review the TCEQ "What's New in Rules" page (https://tceq.texas.gov/rules/whatsnew.html) against all Texas warehouse, trucking, and cargo-handling operations within the next 30 days. Items remain on that page for only 30 days before cycling off; missing a posting means losing early notice of applicable rulemakings. Consequence: failure to track an adopted rule can result in non-compliance from the rule's effective date. *Source: What's New in TCEQ Rules, TCEQ, 2026. https://www.tceq.texas.gov/rules/whatsnew.html*

- **ACTION REQUIRED** — If the workspace operates any fleet vehicle subject to the Texas Inspection and Maintenance (I/M) program in DFW, Houston-Galveston-Brazoria (HGB), Austin-Round Rock (ARR), El Paso, or Bexar County areas, confirm operational readiness for the Bexar County OBD emissions-inspection rollout by **November 1, 2026**. The I/M program in Bexar County begins using OBD inspections on that date as required by the state implementation plan and associated rulemaking (30 TAC Chapter 114, Rule Project Nos. 2022-026-114-AI and 2022-027-SIP-NR). Consequence: vehicles registered in I/M counties that fail to obtain emissions clearance will be out of compliance with fleet registration requirements. *Source: HB 3297 and SB 2102 I/M SIP Revision Adoption Package with Order, TCEQ, 2025. https://www.tceq.texas.gov/downloads/air-quality/mobile-source/25013sip_im_sip_ado.pdf*

- **MONITORING** — Track the newly proposed rulemaking under 30 TAC Chapter 114 (Rule Project No. 2026-003-114-AI) implementing SB 2102 and SB 1729 provisions for initial rental vehicle registration. If the workspace utilizes leased or rented commercial vehicles in Texas ozone nonattainment areas, assess whether the extended three-year initial registration window changes emissions-inspection scheduling for those units. Deadline: comment/adoption timeline not yet published; monitor the TCEQ Rule Projects database actively. *Source: What's New in TCEQ Rules, TCEQ, April–May 2026. https://www.tceq.texas.gov/rules/whatsnew.html*

- **ACTION REQUIRED** — Any workspace facility in Bexar County, Dallas-Fort Worth, or Houston-Galveston-Brazoria that operates stationary sources or uses industrial solvents, surface coating, or degreasing processes must confirm it achieved compliance with 30 TAC Chapters 115 (VOC) and 117 (NOX) ozone nonattainment amendments adopted April 24, 2024. Compliance deadline was **January 1, 2025** (now in force). If a gap audit has not yet been performed, commission one immediately. Consequence: enforcement exposure for continuing non-compliance. *Source: Compliance Deadline Is Jan. 1, 2025 for New and Amended Rules in Ozone Nonattainment Areas, TCEQ, November 2024. https://www.tceq.texas.gov/assistance/resources/the-advocate-1/compliance-deadline-is-jan-1-2025-for-new-and-amended-rules-in-ozone-nonattainment-areas*

- **ACTION REQUIRED** — If the workspace generates or handles hazardous or Class 1 industrial waste at any Texas facility, confirm that the Industrial & Hazardous Waste Annual Waste Summary (AWS) for the prior reporting year has been submitted. All registered generators and reverse distributors with active solid waste registrations must submit an AWS each year. Consequence: delinquent fees or missed submissions trigger loss of good standing and affect processing of future permit applications. *Source: Industrial and Hazardous Waste Reporting, TCEQ, March 2026. https://www.tceq.texas.gov/permitting/registration/ihw/waste_reporting.html*
